channel.title: NPR Topics: Home Page Top Stories
channel.description: Top stories in the U.S. and world news, politics, health, science, business, music, arts and culture. Nonprofit journalism with a mission. This is NPR.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

image.title: NPR Topics: Home Page Top Stories

item.title: French government seeking release of 86-year-old French widow detained by ICE
item.description: The French government is pressing the U.S. Department of Homeland Security to release the 86-year-old French widow of a military veteran from immigration custody in Louisiana after she was detained earlier this month.
item.content:encoded:
The French government is pressing the U.S. Department of Homeland Security to release the 86-year-old French widow of a military veteran from immigration custody in Louisiana after she was detained earlier this month.

(Image credit: Yuki Iwamura)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Bukele signs reforms allowing life prison sentences for people as young as 12
item.description: The reforms signed by Salvadoran President Nayib Bukele would apply to people convicted of committing or being an accomplice to crimes including homicide, femicide, rape and gang membership.
item.content:encoded:
The reforms signed by Salvadoran President Nayib Bukele would apply to people convicted of committing or being an accomplice to crimes including homicide, femicide, rape and gang membership.

(Image credit: Salvador Melendez)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: A Colorado hospital profits from resolving language barriers
item.description: Without qualified interpreters at doctors' offices, non-English speakers can face bad — even fatal — health outcomes. A hospital in rural Colorado is training its existing bilingual staff to address the service gap.
item.content:encoded:
Without qualified interpreters at doctors' offices, non-English speakers can face bad — even fatal — health outcomes. A hospital in rural Colorado is training its existing bilingual staff to address the service gap.

(Image credit: Ashlie Bramley

)
item.dc:creator: Halle Zander

item.title: Whiting Foundation names its 10 emerging authors of 2026
item.description: The annual Whiting Award for Emerging Writers comes with $50,000 to support each winner's work. It's one of the largest prizes granted to promising new authors.
item.content:encoded: The annual Whiting Award for Emerging Writers comes with $50,000 to support each winner's work. It's one of the largest prizes granted to promising new authors.
item.dc:creator: Ivy Buck

item.title: Jury finds that Live Nation acted as a monopoly and overcharged ticket buyers
item.description: An antitrust suit alleged that the company unfairly controlled too much of the live music industry at the expense of venues, artists and fans. The decision could reshape the industry.
item.content:encoded:
An antitrust suit alleged that the company unfairly controlled too much of the live music industry at the expense of venues, artists and fans. The decision could reshape the industry.

(Image credit: Patrick T. Fallon)
item.dc:creator: Isabella Gomez Sarmiento

item.title: Middle East conflict causes a fluoride shortage for US drinking water
item.description: Some U.S. water systems are cutting back on fluoride because of a key chemical is in short supply. Israel is one of its main producers.
item.content:encoded:
Some U.S. water systems are cutting back on fluoride because of a key chemical is in short supply. Israel is one of its main producers.

(Image credit: Joe Raedle)
item.dc:creator: Pien Huang

item.title: What Orban's loss means for far right movements around the world
item.description: The defeat of Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orban at the polls is fueling questions about what's in store for far-right movements in other countries that exploit extremist conspiracies to win.
item.content:encoded: The defeat of Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orban at the polls is fueling questions about what's in store for far-right movements in other countries that exploit extremist conspiracies to win.
item.dc:creator: Odette Yousef

item.title: Popes have spoken out on politics before. But with Trump and Pope Leo it's different
item.description: President Trump's attacks on Pope Leo are unprecedented, religious experts told NPR. Here's how the situation differs from other popes' political critiques.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump's attacks on Pope Leo are unprecedented, religious experts told NPR. Here's how the situation differs from other popes' political critiques.

(Image credit: Alberto Pizzoli)
item.dc:creator: Ava Berger

item.title: Exploring the green side of Rio de Janeiro: a vast urban rain forest
item.description: Rio de Janeiro in Brazil is home to the world's largest urban rain forest. Woven among the city's bustling neighborhoods are soaring tree canopies, waterfalls, and miles of hiking trails.
item.content:encoded:
Rio de Janeiro in Brazil is home to the world's largest urban rain forest. Woven among the city's bustling neighborhoods are soaring tree canopies, waterfalls, and miles of hiking trails.

(Image credit: Brian Mann)
item.dc:creator: Brian Mann

item.title: Actor Amanda Peet says she's 'cancer-free and extremely lucky'
item.description: Peet is always waiting for the other shoe to drop. Except last year there seemed to be three different shoes, as she faced her parents' deaths and a breast cancer diagnosis.
item.content:encoded:
Peet is always waiting for the other shoe to drop. Except last year there seemed to be three different shoes, as she faced her parents' deaths and a breast cancer diagnosis.

(Image credit: Richard Shotwell)
item.dc:creator: Terry Gross
